content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 13 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 106r–107v | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_013_page_001.jpg, DEU_013_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 152

---

1 If a prophet arises among you, or a dreamer of dreams, and gives you a sign or a wonder,

2 and the sign or the wonder comes to pass, of which he spoke to you, saying: "Let us go after other gods" — gods whom you have not known — "and let us serve them,"

3 you shall not listen to the words of that prophet or to that dreamer of dreams, for the LORD your God is testing you, to know whether you love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

4 After the LORD your God you shall walk, and him you shall fear, and his commandments you shall keep, and his voice you shall obey, and him you shall serve, and to him you shall hold fast.

5 But that prophet or that dreamer of dreams shall be put to death, because he spoke rebellion against the LORD your God — who brought you out of the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slavery — to drive you from the way in which the LORD your God commanded you to walk. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

6 If your brother — the son of your mother — or your son, or your daughter, or the wife of your bosom, or your companion who is as your own soul, entices you secretly, saying: "Let us go and serve other gods" — gods whom you have not known, you or your fathers,

7 some of the gods of the peoples who are around you, near you or far from you, from one end of the earth to the other end of the earth —

8 you shall not consent to him and you shall not listen to him, and your eye shall not pity him, and you shall not spare him, and you shall not conceal him.

9 But you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be first against him to put him to death, and afterward the hand of all the people.

10 And you shall stone him with stones, that he may die, because he sought to drive you away from the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, from the house of slavery.

11 And all Israel shall hear and fear, and shall never again do such an evil thing as this in your midst.

12 If you hear, in one of your cities which the LORD your God is giving you to dwell there, saying:

13 "Worthless men have gone out from among you and have driven away the inhabitants of their city, saying: 'Let us go and serve other gods' — gods whom you have not known" —

14 then you shall inquire and investigate and ask diligently; and behold, if the matter is true and the thing certain, that such an abomination has been done in your midst,

15 you shall surely strike the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, devoting it to destruction — it and all that is in it and its livestock — with the edge of the sword.

16 And all its spoil you shall gather into the middle of its public square, and you shall burn with fire the city and all its spoil, as a whole burnt offering to the LORD your God; and it shall be a ruin heap forever; it shall not be built again.

17 And nothing of the devoted things shall cling to your hand, so that the LORD may turn from his burning anger and show you mercy, and have compassion on you and multiply you, just as he swore to your fathers —

18 when you obey the voice of the LORD your God, keeping all his commandments that I am commanding you today, to do what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

**Verse 1 — navi / cholem chalom:** "Prophet" for *navi* and "dreamer of dreams" for *cholem chalom* retained as distinct categories. The LXX uses *prophetes* and *enypniazomenos enypnion* — close to the MT distinction.

**Verse 3 — massah (testing):** "Testing you" for *menasseh*; the same root as Massah in the wilderness narrative. The LXX renders *ekpeirazei* (is testing/trying). The theological point is significant: a sign coming true does not validate the prophet if it leads away from YHWH.

**Verse 5 — dibbel 'al-YHWH (spoke rebellion):** "Spoke rebellion against the LORD" for *dibber sarah 'al-YHWH*. LXX reads *apeplanesen apo kyriou* ("led astray from the LORD") — paraphrastic. MT's *sarah* (rebellion, apostasy) is retained literally.

**Verse 5 — u-bi'arta ha-ra' (purge the evil):** The formulaic phrase marking the end of legal sanctions in Deuteronomy; rendered consistently as "purge the evil from your midst." LXX: *aphaireite ton poneron ex hymon auton*.

**Verse 6 — 'eshet cheqekha (wife of your bosom):** "Wife of your bosom" retained as formal equivalent of the Hebrew idiom indicating the most intimate companion. LXX: *gynaika ton en kolpois sou*.

**Verse 9 — harog tahargennu (you shall surely kill him):** Emphatic infinitive absolute construction; rendered "you shall surely kill him." LXX: *thanato apokteneis auton*.

**Verse 13 — benei beliya'al (sons of worthlessness):** "Worthless men" following established convention from earlier books. The idiom *benei beliya'al* literally means "sons of worthlessness/destruction"; "worthless men" preserves the social condemnation while being idiomatic English. LXX: *andres paranomoi* ("lawless men").

**Verse 15 — cherem (devoted to destruction):** "Devoting it to destruction" for *cherem* — the sacred ban. Used here for an apostate Israelite city. LXX: *anathema* — which is the Greek term that enters NT usage. The word implies both total destruction and the setting apart of something as utterly belonging to God's judgment.

**Verse 16 — kalil la-YHWH (whole burnt offering):** "Whole burnt offering" for *kalil*; an unusual application of sacrificial vocabulary to a destroyed city. LXX: *holokautoma* — exact equivalent.

**Verse numbering:** The Hebrew (MT/LC) and English chapter 13 begins at "If a prophet arises" — some versional traditions number the *shema* command at the chapter boundary differently. The Vaticanus (LXX) page 152 opens mid-chapter with material from chapters 13–14, confirming the chapter runs 1–18 in MT numbering. LOGOS follows the MT/LC enumeration throughout.

*— LOGOS*
